# Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ: FRMI) — News & Price-Impact Summary

**Window: Jun 24 – Jul 1, 2026 · Last ~$9.16 (mid-$9s), choppy and roughly flat-to-lower on the week after a mid-June run above $10 · Net read: a live boardroom control fight is the dominant driver, while a steady drip of Project Matador construction deals supplies the bull case — the latest ~3% session drop looks sector-driven, not stock-specific.**

## What's moving the stock

The center of gravity for FRMI right now is not the campus in Amarillo — it is the escalating governance war between the current board and co-founder/former CEO Toby Neugebauer, who is the company's largest shareholder. Neugebauer is soliciting written shareholder consents to call a special meeting, seize board control, and potentially push a rapid sale of the company; the sitting board is fighting back with a Consent Revocation campaign and repeated warnings that handing him control would "jeopardize shareholder value." This turns FRMI into a binary situation: if Neugebauer prevails, the stock likely trades on buyout speculation; if the board holds, it trades on execution of its "Fermi 2.0" plan.

Two developments inside the window tilted this fight toward Neugebauer. First, on **June 26, 2026**, a court granted him **expedited discovery**, ordering Fermi to produce documents and testimony tied to a disputed bylaw — a procedural win that lends his campaign legitimacy. Court filings peg his beneficial ownership at roughly **146.5 million shares** (including ~44.7 million held through Vicksburg), an unusually large insider stake that makes his consent drive credible. Second, **two proxy advisory firms recommended that shareholders CONSENT** to calling the special meeting — an important third-party endorsement that shifts leverage toward the insurgent. Neugebauer then announced a **town hall webinar with analysts on June 30**, pressing his transparency narrative and challenging the Perry-led board to answer questions publicly.

The mechanism tying this to the share price is counterparty risk. Fermi's own defense rests on partners and lenders tying their support to "stable governance" with Neugebauer out of control. So the market is caught between two readings: a Neugebauer win could unlock buyout value, but it could also spook the very financing and commercial partners the capital-intensive project depends on. That tension — not any single press release — is what keeps FRMI a high-beta trading vehicle (reported beta ~2.39) rather than a clean fundamental story.

On price, the beta check matters. FRMI closed the window near **$9.16, down about 3%** on the latest session, but that move landed on a day when the broader REIT/data-center complex sold off hard — Digital Realty, Equinix, Crown Castle and American Tower were all down roughly 3–6% the same session. That argues the ~3% dip was **sector/macro-driven, not a FRMI-specific reaction** to any headline. Over the full week the stock was roughly flat-to-lower, digesting a mid-June surge that had carried it above $10 on OpenAI data-center optimism (a catalyst that predates this window).

## Other developments

**Primoris tapped for phase-one power plant construction.** On **June 30, 2026**, Fermi announced it selected **Primoris Energy Services** to engineer and construct the "balance of plant" for the first six **SGT-800 gas turbines** in Project Matador's phase-one power buildout — framed by the company as "another decisive step in Fermi's 2.0 execution strategy." Notably, the operational good news did not lift the stock; shares traded near $9.45 and slipped on the session, suggesting the governance overhang and a sector selloff dominated. *Source: Fermi press release / Investing.com / Alliance News · Jun 30, 2026 · operational positive, no confirmed upside move (stock flat-to-lower on the day).*

**TSK selected for phase-two early works.** On **June 25, 2026**, Fermi said it chose **TSK** (described as Spain's largest EPC firm) to deliver early-works engineering for **three Siemens SGT6-5000F turbines**, accelerating engineering and permitting for Project Matador's second phase. This is the second EPC-partner announcement in the window, reinforcing the "execution is progressing" narrative the board is running on. *Source: Fermi press release (PRNewswire) / Stocktwits · Jun 25, 2026 · anticipated/positive, no isolated move confirmed.*

**Neugebauer's chairman gambit via Double Eagle.** Neugebauer published an open letter proposing that **John Sellers, a Double Eagle Energy principal, serve as chairman** — a move to signal a credible alternative slate. Double Eagle publicly acknowledged awareness of the letter. This feeds the control-fight narrative rather than moving the stock on its own. *Source: Double Eagle statement / stockanalysis.com filings feed · late June 2026 · governance, noise-to-anticipated.*

**Analyst tone had softened just before the window.** For context, around **June 22–23** (just outside the strict 7-day window), **Stifel cut its target to $17 from $29** while keeping a Buy, citing contract delays; **Citizens reiterated Market Outperform at $30**; and **Evercore ISI** had earlier downgraded to In Line. Analysts remain split, with a consensus "Buy" and a ~$19 average target. *Source: Investing.com / CNN analyst feed · Jun 22–23, 2026 · mixed sentiment, context only.*

## Quick reference

| Date | Headline | Impact |
|------|----------|--------|
| Jun 25, 2026 | Fermi selects TSK for early-works engineering on 3 Siemens SGT6-5000F turbines (phase two) | Positive/execution; no isolated move confirmed |
| Jun 26, 2026 | Court grants Neugebauer expedited discovery in bylaw dispute | Governance negative for board; incremental |
| ~Jun 29, 2026 | Two proxy advisory firms recommend shareholders CONSENT to special meeting | Governance positive for insurgent; raises deal odds |
| Jun 30, 2026 | Neugebauer hosts town-hall webinar with analysts | Governance/narrative; no confirmed move |
| Jun 30, 2026 | Fermi selects Primoris for balance-of-plant on first six SGT-800 turbines (phase one) | Operational positive; stock flat-to-lower (sector selloff) |
| Jun 30–Jul 1 | FRMI ~$9.16, ~-3% session alongside 3–6% drops across REIT/data-center peers | Macro/sector-driven, not stock-specific |

## What to watch

Net read: FRMI is trading as a governance event stock — the Neugebauer consent battle now has court and proxy-advisor momentum behind it, while a stream of EPC/turbine deals keeps the operational bull case alive but has not, on its own, been enough to move shares.

- **ANTICIPATED:** Resolution of the consent solicitation / whether a **special shareholder meeting** is formally called — the single biggest binary.
- **ANTICIPATED:** Fallout from the **June 30 Neugebauer town hall** and any board rebuttal or additional proxy-advisor guidance.
- **ANTICIPATED:** Court proceedings following the **June 26 expedited-discovery** order (document production, testimony).
- **ANTICIPATED:** Whether financing/commercial partners reaffirm or condition support amid the control fight.
- **ANTICIPATED:** Next earnings report scheduled for **Sep 2, 2026**.

**Most important thing to watch:** whether Neugebauer converts proxy-advisor and court momentum into enough consents to call the special meeting — that outcome flips the stock between a "buyout speculation" story and an "execute Fermi 2.0" story.
